The Internship (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $44,672,764Details
International Box Office $49,000,000
Worldwide Box Office $93,672,764
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,256,373
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,207,411
Total $13,463,784
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Billy and Nick are salesmen whose careers have been torpedoed by the digital world. Trying to prove they are not obsolete, they defy the odds by talking their way into a coveted internship at Google, along with a battalion of brilliant college students. But, gaining entrance to this utopia is only half the battle. Now they must compete with a group of the nation’s most elite, tech-savvy geniuses to prove that necessity really is the mother of re-invention.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$17,325,307 (38.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.58 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:47.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$58,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,366 opening theaters/3,399 max. theaters, 3.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $65,717,868

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 7th, 2013 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: August 30th, 2013 (Wide) (Brazil)
September 12th, 2013 (Wide) (Malaysia)
December 20th, 2013 (Wide) (Venezuela)
Video Release: October 22nd, 2013 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexuality, some crude humor, partying and language.
(Rating bulletin 2271, 5/8/2013)
Running Time: 119 minutes

Synopsis

Billy and Nick are salesmen whose careers have been torpedoed by the digital world. Trying to prove they are not obsolete, they defy the odds by talking their way into a coveted internship at Google, along with a battalion of brilliant college students. But, gaining entrance to this utopia is only half the battle. Now they must compete with a group of the nation’s most elite, tech-savvy geniuses to prove that necessity really is the mother of re-invention.
